criteria.criterion: ReconcilerConfig.backend exists in src/rebar/_config_schema.py, defaults to "jira", and a value outside the _as_choice set is rejected at config-load with a ConfigError naming the allowed keys

select_backend(config) accepts the app-level Config, reads config.reconciler.backend, and returns a Backend; the default returns a JiraBackend whose transport is an AcliClient built from resolve_jira_settings() values
register is idempotent (same key+factory = no-op; conflicting factory raises) and _reset_registry_for_test round-trips registry state
Teeth: with _reset_registry_for_test, a FakeBackend registered under a test key makes select_backend return that fake, and reverting the registration makes the same lookup raise

citation.description: The four S3 deliverable files are present and correctly implemented. Consumer files (applier.py, fetcher.py, run_differs.py) do use select_backend, but per the ticket's explicit scope and the comment's attestation (commit 245a086af touching only the 4 expected src/ files + 3 test files), those rewires are attributed to S4 (commit eef1afa1f). The current codebase state reflects the stacked epic's cumulative main. Git history is not directly inspectable via file tools, but the comment provides a concrete per-commit diff attestation naming the specific files and commit ID.

criteria.criterion: No existing consumer (_load_acli, the AcliClient construction sites, the differ/apply modules) is modified — verified by git diff --name-only origin/main listing only the four expected files + tests
Every src/rebar/**/*.py stays within the 800-LOC cap — verified by pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py
